{{Quote|Talandro Starlyte…Let's see. How about a sithspawn in nerfherder's clothing?|Cohden K'Reye|<ref name="WHSV" />}}
'''Talandro Starlyte''' was most famous for renting the [[Starlyte Station|Gateway Space Station]] from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], which he renamed Starlyte Station. His various criminal enterprises were for the most part overlooked by the Empire. This was due to the large bribes he gave to the station's [[Imperial Liaison]], [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Arissa Fawn]].

==Biography==
Not much was known of his history and some believed that even his name was an alias. He claimed to be a born on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Before he acquired the [[Starlyte Station]], he spent time working in [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]], where he met [[Scizzic]], whom he hired to repair the [[Space station/Legends|space station]]. During his time on [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]] he was also rumored to have done some business with [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]].

Starlyte became one of the premier fences in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. His station was infamous for its [[Black market/Legends|black market]] operations. He kept contact with criminals all over the galaxy, including [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], [[Ploovo Two-For-One/Legends|Ploovo Two-For-One]] and the [[Sabrin Ring]]. Although he was willing to deal with almost any criminal, he steered clear of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] or [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] operations. He also [[kidnapped]] and [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] visitors to his station and impounded their equipment and [[Starship/Legends|starships]]. Most of the slaves were turned over to the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Dahz Thulaka]] who conducted illegal experiments on them. The ones he could not use were sold to the [[Karazak Slavers' Cooperative/Legends|Karazak Slavers Cooperative]].

==Personality and traits==
Starlyte was a very shady individual. He danced a fine line between good and evil in a moral grey zone. His merchandise was not cheap but neither was it junk nor overpriced compared to other black markets. A shrewd businessman, Starlyte was quick with a smile, tenacious when he desired something and overall a consummate rogue. He showed a great disdain for [[Corellian/Legends|Corellians]]. He was considered handsome with a ponytail of straight black hair and rugged good looks. He kept his appearance immaculate and was always draped in expensive clothes.

==Skills and abilities==
A jack of all trades, Starlyte had talent to mastery in a wide variety of skills. He was competent with his fists and with a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], he could pilot [[Transport/Legends|transports]] and [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] and could [[Slicer/Legends|slice]] his way past security. His true talents were in black market operations where he truly excelled.

==Equipment==
Beneath his expensive clothing and jewellery, Starlyte was well prepared in case of trouble. He typically carried a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] and a [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]] as well as a [[Vibroknife/Legends|vibroknife]]. He also had a voice-locked [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]].

==Behind the scenes==
Talandro Starlyte was designed by [[Paul Danner]] and [[Bill Smith]] for ''[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]'', a supplement for the ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' that was published by [[West End Games]] in [[1997]]. He was illustrated by [[Joe Corroney]].
